issues.message: V0-V1 move in parallel Major 3rd (+1 oct) from beat 13 to 14 (1 transition, strength 2.2 from the ratio table): a rooted interval, so common-fate motion merges the voices into one bass-rooted stream. Legitimate as a deliberate handoff or doubling; otherwise restore independence with contrary or oblique motion.

V0-V1 travel in parallel Minor 3rd (+1 oct) from beat 29 to 31 (2 transitions, strength 2.2 from the ratio table): the interval's implied fundamental is displaced from the lower note (ratio denominator has an odd factor), so the unvoiced root travels in parallel with the pair. Break the traveling fourth-class motion, or voice the root so the displacement is intentional.

V0-V1 co-move in the same direction from beat 32 to 36.5 (7 co-movements, 2 locked steps): the voices share one directional predictor and collapse toward a single stream even without parallel perfects. Give one voice contrary or oblique cells, or make the doubling deliberate.

Voice pairs share one attack clock for 62% of their time (V0-V1 0.60, V0-V2 0.36, V1-V2 0.90): counter-lines are harmonizing rather than moving in independent rhythmic strata. Deliberate in homophony/chorale textures; in counterpoint, put the countersubject at a different rate (double-speed cells or augmentation) attacking in the other voice's gaps.
